cat /etc/init.d/cron:
#!/bin/sh

### BEGIN INIT INFO
# Provides:          cron
# Required-Start:    $remote_fs $syslog
# Required-Stop:     $remote_fs $syslog
# Default-Start:     2 3 4 5
# Default-Stop:      0 1 6
# Short-Description: regular background program processing daemon
# Description:       cron is a standard UNIX program that runs user-specified
#                    programs at periodic scheduled times
### END INIT INFO

PATH=/sbin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin

test -x /usr/sbin/cron || exit 0

. /lib/lsb/init-functions

CRON_NAME=cron
CRON_DAEMON=/usr/sbin/cron
CRON_OPTS=
PIDFILE=/var/run/crond.pid

test -f $PIDFILE && read crond_pid < $PIDFILE
if [ -n "$crond_pid" ] && (ps -p $crond_pid | grep -q cron); then
    log_action_begin_msg "Already running."
    log_action_end_msg 0
    exit 0
fi

cron_start() {
    start-stop-daemon --start --quiet --oknodo --exec $CRON_DAEMON -- $CRON_OPTS
}

cron_stop() {
    start-stop-daemon --stop --quiet --oknodo --exec $CRON_DAEMON
}

case "$1" in
    start)
        log_daemon_msg "Starting periodic command scheduler" "$CRON_NAME"
        cron_start
        log_end_msg $?
        ;;
    stop)
        log_daemon_msg "Stopping periodic command scheduler" "$CRON_NAME"
        cron_stop
        log_end_msg $?
        ;;
    restart|force-reload)
        log_daemon_msg "Restarting periodic command scheduler" "$CRON_NAME"
        cron_stop
        sleep 1
        cron_start
        log_end_msg $?
        ;;
    status)
        status_of_proc -p $PIDFILE $CRON_DAEMON "$CRON_NAME" && exit 0 || exit $?
        ;;
    *)
        echo "Usage: /etc/init.d/cron {start|stop|restart|force-reload|status}"
        exit 1
        ;;
esac

exit 0
